title: PhD
biography: Susumu Tonegawa is a Japanese molecular biologist who won the Nobel Prize in 1987 for discovering the genetic mechanism of antibody diversity (V(D)J recombination). He later pivoted to neuroscience, making breakthrough discoveries in memory engram cells at MIT.
aiSummary: Susumu Tonegawa is an immunology and neuroscience specialist with an H-index of 130 at MIT. Nobel Prize 1987 for discovering the genetic mechanism of antibody diversity. Based in Cambridge, United States.

timeline.title: Discovered V(D)J recombination
timeline.description: Demonstrated how antibody genes rearrange to produce diverse antibodies from limited genetic material

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Sole recipient for discovery of genetic principle for generation of antibody diversity

timeline.title: Pivoted to neuroscience
timeline.description: Established neuroscience laboratory at MIT to study memory and learning

timeline.title: Identified memory engram cells
timeline.description: Used optogenetics to activate specific memory traces in the hippocampus
